title: StrategyProjection
description: DRAFT v0 — per-ad marketing-strategy projection. TEXT-ONLY: derived from completed analysis artifacts (ad-type, copy, layout, visual, intent, bindings) + the ad's own advertiser/product context — NEVER the image. Ring 2, read on the AD'S OWN product selling-point (not ours). Projects (does not re-classify): funnel_intent⟵intent.funnel_stage, benefit_vector⟵intent.appeal coarsening. grounds_in cites knowledge/reference/ad-strategy-taxonomy.md (Sheth-Newman-Gross 1991 / Lavidge-Steiner 1961 / Petty-Cacioppo 1986 ELM / Griffin-Hauser 1993 VoC / Goldenberg-Mazursky-Solomon 1999).

advertiser_context.description: The ad's OWN advertiser/product context (from collection metadata), so the projection reads 'company X's product Y ad creates purchase-reason Z' — NOT a judgement against our product.

benefit_vector.description: The purchase reason the ad creates (Sheth-Newman-Gross 1991 + Park-Jaworski-MacInnis 1986; cost←Zeithaml 1988, trust←perceived-risk). `unclear` = insufficient evidence.

funnel_intent.description: The buyer-readiness stage the ad targets (Lavidge & Steiner 1961 Hierarchy of Effects; retention=loyalty-loop). Projection of intent.funnel_stage. `unclear` = insufficient evidence.

first_cognition.description: Does the ad communicate in the first glance (Petty & Cacioppo 1986 ELM low-elaboration / ad comprehension). total_score MUST equal the sum of the eight 0-2 sub-scores.

customer_language.description: Whose words the copy uses (Griffin & Hauser 1993 Voice of the Customer + JTBD).

generation_reusability.description: What can be reused vs must not be copied (Goldenberg-Mazursky-Solomon 1999 creativity templates: reuse the abstract device, never the competitor's specific content).

grounds_in.description: REQUIRED provenance — the ad-strategy-taxonomy.md source(s) this projection rests on (e.g. 'Sheth-Newman-Gross 1991 functional; Lavidge-Steiner 1961 comparison'). A projection with no basis is a defect.
